steps.title: Substitute the time
steps.content:
The equation is $s=40+3t$, where $t$ is seconds after acceleration begins. For $5$ seconds, use $t=5$:
$$s=40+3(5)$$

steps.title: Compute the speed
steps.content:
Evaluate the expression:
$$s=40+15=55$$
The result is the speed in miles per hour after $5$ seconds; using only $40$, $43$, or $45$ would not add the full increase.
The answer is D.
